title: Username enumeration via inbox endpoint status codes
description:
server.rs:532-535: For Follow activities targeting a local user, the handler returns `StatusCode::NOT_FOUND` (404) if the username does not exist, and `StatusCode::ACCEPTED` (202) if it does and the follow was inserted.

An attacker can enumerate valid usernames by sending unsigned Follow activities to /inbox and observing:
- 404: username does not exist
- 202: username exists

The webfinger endpoint (server.rs:174-180) has the same behavior (404 vs 200), but webfinger enumeration requires knowing the domain. The inbox endpoint accepts arbitrary `object` URIs and the response discriminates on existence.

Rate limiting is bypassable (broadside-0e74.7), so high-speed enumeration is feasible.

Mitigation: return ACCEPTED regardless of whether the username exists (accept-and-discard pattern), or rate-limit more aggressively and return ACCEPTED for unknown users.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
